bhavas.name: Non-violence
bhavas.description: Compassion and respect for all living beings

bhavas.name: Truthfulness
bhavas.description: Commitment to speaking and living the truth

bhavas.name: Non-stealing
bhavas.description: Respect for what belongs to others

bhavas.name: Righteousness
bhavas.description: Faithful performance of one's duties

bhavas.name: Faith
bhavas.description: Belief in karma and cosmic order

bhavas.name: Forgiveness
bhavas.description: Ability to remain calm and forgiving

bhavas.name: Patience
bhavas.description: Endurance and steadfastness

bhavas.name: Good conduct
bhavas.description: Discipline and adherence to ethical behaviour

bhavas.name: Egolessness
bhavas.description: Humility and absence of pride

bhavas.name: Intellect
bhavas.description: Strong retention, memory, and learning

bhavas.name: Selfless service
bhavas.description: Generosity without expectation of return

bhavas.name: Purity
bhavas.description: Cleanliness of mind, body, and environment

bhavas.name: Impatience
bhavas.description: Restlessness when results are delayed

bhavas.name: Pride
bhavas.description: Attachment to personal achievements

bhavas.name: Ego
bhavas.description: Strong sense of self-importance

bhavas.name: Materialism
bhavas.description: Desire for possessions and luxury

bhavas.name: Anger
bhavas.description: Frustration and irritability

bhavas.name: Competitiveness
bhavas.description: Constant comparison and desire to outperform

bhavas.name: Cunning
bhavas.description: Preference for shortcuts and manipulation

bhavas.name: Stubbornness
bhavas.description: Insistence on having things one's own way

bhavas.name: Exaggeration
bhavas.description: Tendency to overstate experiences

bhavas.name: Outward aggression
bhavas.description: Loud expression of frustration and anger

bhavas.name: Excess sleep
bhavas.description: Preference for sleeping over productive activity

bhavas.name: Laziness
bhavas.description: Habitual missing of commitments and deadlines

bhavas.name: Lack of intellect
bhavas.description: Avoidance of learning and intellectual growth

bhavas.name: Mental avoidance
bhavas.description: Avoidance of creative or effortful thinking

bhavas.name: Forgetfulness
bhavas.description: Chronic forgetfulness and poor memory

brahma.name: Brahma Kaya
brahma.description: You are a natural seeker of truth and knowledge. Your mind is drawn to understanding the deeper meaning behind everything  - why things work the way they do, what is right, what is just. You process the world through intellect and discernment, often seeing patterns and connections others miss.
behavioral_patterns: You're the person friends come to when they need honest, unbiased advice  - and you give it even when it's uncomfortable
You lose track of time reading, learning, or diving deep into a subject that fascinates you
You feel physically uncomfortable when you have to lie or bend the truth, even in small social situations
You often think several steps ahead in conversations, which can make you seem quiet  - you're actually processing deeply
You'd rather be respected for your knowledge than liked for being agreeable
shadows: Can become detached or overly cerebral
May judge others who don't value truth as deeply
Can appear cold when you're actually processing emotions internally
brahma.relationship_style: You value depth over breadth in relationships. You have few close friends but those bonds are built on mutual respect and intellectual honesty. You struggle with people who are superficial or dishonest.
brahma.work_style: You thrive in roles requiring analysis, strategy, and ethical decision-making. You're the one who asks the hard questions in meetings that everyone else was thinking but afraid to voice.

mahendra.name: Mahendra Kaya
mahendra.description: You carry a natural authority that others sense immediately. Your strength isn't loud or aggressive  - it comes from discipline, consistency, and an unshakable commitment to doing things properly. You lead by example, not by force.
behavioral_patterns: You have routines and systems for everything  - and you feel genuinely unsettled when they're disrupted
People naturally look to you to take charge in group situations, even when you haven't volunteered
You keep promises as if they were sacred  - breaking a commitment feels like a personal failure
You respect hierarchy and tradition, but only when the people in authority have earned it
You push through discomfort with a discipline that others find impressive and sometimes intimidating
shadows: Can be rigid when flexibility is needed
Difficulty delegating  - you believe you'll do it better
May suppress emotions in favor of 'being strong'
Can hold others to impossibly high standards
mahendra.relationship_style: You're the rock in your relationships. People rely on you, and you take that responsibility seriously. But you sometimes forget that vulnerability is also a form of strength.
mahendra.work_style: You excel in structured environments with clear expectations. You're the person who arrives early, stays late, and never misses a deadline  - but you struggle when rules feel arbitrary.

varuna.name: Varuna Kaya
varuna.description: Your defining quality is an almost infinite capacity for patience and forgiveness. Where others react, you absorb. Where others hold grudges, you let go. This isn't weakness  - it's a profound inner stability that allows you to remain calm when everything around you is chaotic.
behavioral_patterns: You're the one who de-escalates fights  - between friends, family members, even strangers
You can sit with uncomfortable silence without feeling the need to fill it
People have told you you're 'too nice' or 'too forgiving'  - you don't see it as a problem
You notice environmental details others miss  - a messy room genuinely affects your mood
You process anger slowly  - you might not realize you're upset until hours or days later
shadows: Others may take advantage of your forgiving nature
Tendency to suppress anger rather than process it
May stay in harmful situations too long out of patience
varuna.relationship_style: You create a sense of safety for everyone around you. Your relationships are warm and steady, but you need to watch for patterns where you give more than you receive.
varuna.work_style: You're the calming presence in stressful teams. You handle customer complaints, mediate conflicts, and maintain quality  - but you may not advocate loudly enough for your own advancement.

kaubera.name: Kaubera Kaya
kaubera.description: Generosity isn't something you practice  - it's how you naturally move through the world. You share time, resources, attention, and energy without keeping score. Your sense of abundance comes from within, not from what you own.
behavioral_patterns: You're the first person to offer help  - and the last person to ask for it
You feel genuine joy when giving gifts or treating someone to a meal
You treat the security guard and the CEO with the same respect  - status hierarchies confuse you
You've been called 'too generous' by people who worry you'll be taken advantage of
You feel uncomfortable with excess  - you'd rather share than accumulate
shadows: May give to the point of self-depletion
Can feel resentful when generosity isn't reciprocated
kaubera.relationship_style: Your relationships are characterized by warmth and generosity. You remember birthdays, you check in on people, you show up. But you need to learn that receiving is also a gift you can give others.
kaubera.work_style: You're the team player everyone loves working with. You share credit, mentor juniors, and contribute to team culture. Your challenge is ensuring your contributions are recognized and compensated fairly.

gandharva.name: Gandharva Kaya
gandharva.description: You experience the world through beauty and aesthetics. Your environment deeply affects your inner state  - a well-organized space lifts you up, while chaos drains you. You have a natural drive to make things better, cleaner, more beautiful.
behavioral_patterns: You notice when a picture frame is slightly crooked  - and it bothers you until you fix it
You put effort into your appearance, your workspace, your home  - not for others, but because it matters to you
You adapt quickly to new situations and environments  - change doesn't scare you, it excites you
You find purpose and meaning in your daily activities, even mundane ones
You believe that hard work always pays off, and you live by that belief
shadows: Can become perfectionist about appearance and environment
gandharva.relationship_style: You bring beauty and order to your relationships. You plan thoughtful dates, create lovely home environments, and put effort into maintaining connections. Your partner needs to appreciate the care you put into these details.
gandharva.work_style: You thrive in creative, aesthetic, or design-oriented roles. You're the person who makes the presentation look beautiful, who redesigns the workflow to be more elegant.

yamya.name: Yamya Kaya
yamya.description: Justice and fairness are your core operating principles. You have an internal compass that always points toward what is right  - not what is convenient, popular, or easy. You hold yourself to the same standards you expect from others.
behavioral_patterns: You speak up when you see injustice, even when it's socially uncomfortable
You follow rules because you genuinely believe in their purpose, not because you fear punishment
You can't stand hypocrisy  - people who preach one thing and do another frustrate you deeply
You have strong opinions about right and wrong, but you hold them with humility
You take your responsibilities seriously  - 'it's not my job' isn't in your vocabulary
shadows: Can be inflexible about ethical gray areas
May judge others too harshly for moral lapses
Difficulty accepting that not everyone shares your values
Can burn out from carrying too much responsibility
yamya.relationship_style: You're the friend who will tell you the truth even when it hurts. Your loyalty is earned, not given  - but once earned, it's unbreakable. You need partners who match your commitment to honesty and fairness.
yamya.work_style: You're drawn to roles with purpose  - law, education, public service, ethics. You're the person who writes the policy, who ensures compliance, who advocates for the underdog.

rishi.name: Rishi Kaya
rishi.description: Your inner world is vast and rich. You're naturally drawn to spiritual practice, contemplation, and the pursuit of wisdom. You find contentment not in external achievements but in internal understanding. Others often don't see the depth of your inner life.
behavioral_patterns: You need regular solitude to recharge  - social situations drain you faster than most people realize
You've always been drawn to spiritual or philosophical questions, even as a child
You don't gossip or envy others  - not because you're trying to be good, but because it genuinely doesn't interest you
You remember things others forget  - details, conversations, knowledge from years ago
You feel most alive during meditation, prayer, or deep contemplation
shadows: Can become too withdrawn from worldly responsibilities
Others may see you as detached or disengaged
May struggle to connect with people who don't share your depth
rishi.relationship_style: You form deep, meaningful bonds with very few people. You need a partner who respects your need for solitude and shares your interest in the deeper questions of life.
rishi.work_style: You excel in research, teaching, counseling, or any role that allows depth over speed. You're not competitive  - you're thorough. Your work has a quality that comes from genuine understanding, not just effort.

asura.name: Asura Kaya
asura.description: You are a force of nature. Your ambition, drive, and self-confidence create a magnetic presence that draws people in and pushes boundaries. You don't just want success  - you want to dominate your field, be the best, leave a legacy.
behavioral_patterns: You walk into a room and automatically assess the power dynamics  - who's in charge, who's a threat, who's useful
You set goals that others call unrealistic, then hit them  - that's your fuel
You've been called 'too much' or 'too intense'  - you wear it as a badge of honor
You need to be in control. Delegating feels like losing grip, even when you know it's necessary
You measure your worth by your achievements, possessions, and influence
strengths: Can mobilize and lead large groups
shadows: Ego can blind you to your own mistakes
Difficulty being vulnerable or asking for help
May alienate people with your intensity
asura.relationship_style: You attract people with your energy and confidence, but intimacy requires vulnerability  - your weakest muscle. You need a partner who is strong enough not to be overwhelmed by you, and honest enough to call you out.
asura.work_style: You're the entrepreneur, the empire builder, the CEO. You create, you scale, you dominate. Your challenge is learning that control and success aren't the same thing.

sarpa.name: Sarpa Kaya
sarpa.description: Your emotional intensity is your defining feature. You feel everything deeply  - love, anger, betrayal, loyalty. When you're calm, you're magnetic and perceptive. When you're triggered, the force of your reaction surprises even you.
behavioral_patterns: You remember every slight, every betrayal  - your emotional memory is extraordinarily detailed
Your anger doesn't build slowly  - it strikes fast and hard, then you regret it
You can read people's true intentions with uncanny accuracy  - you just 'know' when someone is lying
Traffic jams, rude people, broken promises  - small things trigger disproportionate fury
You hold grudges longer than you'd like to admit, even when you know it hurts you
strengths: Fierce loyalty to those you love
shadows: Your intensity exhausts people around you
sarpa.relationship_style: You love hard and protect fiercely. Your relationships are intense  - there's no 'casual' in your vocabulary. You need a partner who can handle your emotional depth without being consumed by it.
sarpa.work_style: You're at your best in high-stakes environments where emotional intelligence matters  - negotiation, crisis management, sales. Your challenge is managing reactive anger in professional settings.

shakuna.name: Shakuna Kaya
shakuna.description: You are restless, quick, and always scanning for the next opportunity. Your mind moves faster than most  - jumping from idea to idea, plan to plan. You thrive on novelty and wilt in routine.
behavioral_patterns: You have 12 browser tabs open, 3 half-finished projects, and a new idea you're excited about
You make decisions quickly  - sometimes too quickly  - and deal with consequences later
Routine physically drains you. You need variety, change, stimulation
You're competitive not out of malice but because winning gives you the dopamine hit you crave
You need external motivation to finish things  - starting is easy, completing is the struggle
shakuna.relationship_style: You're exciting but unpredictable. Your partners love your energy but struggle with your restlessness. You need someone who keeps things interesting without enabling your avoidance of depth.
shakuna.work_style: Startups, sales, trading, journalism  - anything fast-paced with variety. You're the person who thrives in chaos. Your Achilles heel is the boring-but-important maintenance work.

rakshasa.name: Rakshasa Kaya
rakshasa.description: You have a raw, unfiltered intensity that others either admire or fear. You don't tolerate incompetence, dishonesty, or weakness  - in yourself or others. Your standards are impossibly high, and you enforce them relentlessly.
behavioral_patterns: You say what others are thinking but afraid to say  - and you don't sugarcoat it
Waiting in lines, slow service, inefficient processes  - these things make your blood boil
You'd rather do it yourself than explain it to someone who might do it wrong
You've been told you're intimidating. You see it as honesty; others see it as aggression
You respect strength and competence above all else  - titles and seniority mean nothing to you
strengths: Gets results where others give up
shadows: Controls through intimidation rather than inspiration
rakshasa.relationship_style: You need a partner with a thick skin and their own backbone. You respect people who stand up to you. Your love language is 'acts of service'  - you show care by doing, not by saying.
rakshasa.work_style: You're the turnaround specialist, the drill sergeant, the person they call when things are broken and need fixing fast. Your challenge is sustainability  - you burn hot, and you burn out.

paishaca.name: Paishaca Kaya
paishaca.description: You have a complicated relationship with pleasure and excess. You're drawn to sensory experiences  - food, shopping, social media, the next exciting thing. You're aware that some of these patterns don't serve you, but the pull is strong.
behavioral_patterns: You eat when stressed, shop when bored, scroll when anxious  - pleasure is your coping mechanism
You're good at presenting a curated version of yourself  - on social media and in person
You know shortcuts to everything and you're not ashamed to use them
You spend more than you should to maintain a lifestyle that looks good from the outside
You exaggerate stories to make them more interesting  - a little embellishment never hurt anyone, right?
shadows: Curated image vs. authentic self gap
paishaca.relationship_style: You're fun and exciting to be around, but your relationships can be performative. The growth edge is allowing people to see the real you  - not just the curated version.
paishaca.work_style: Marketing, entertainment, hospitality, sales  - anything that leverages your social skills and love of experience. Your challenge is building substance underneath the style.

preta.name: Preta Kaya
preta.description: There's a hunger in you that never quite gets satisfied. You compare, you evaluate, you measure yourself against others. It's not malice  - it's a deep-seated feeling that you should be further along, that others have something you're missing.
behavioral_patterns: You check how many likes your posts get. You notice who got promoted before you. You compare salaries
Even after getting what you wanted, there's a brief moment of satisfaction followed by 'what's next?'
You talk about your achievements because you need external validation to feel they're real
You feel a pang when a friend succeeds  - you're happy for them, but there's a whisper of 'why not me?'
You set expectations for others and feel crushed when they don't meet them
strengths: Observant and analytical about social dynamics
High expectations lead to high output
Hunger for growth keeps you moving forward
shadows: Comparison is the thief of your joy
Others feel they can never be enough for you
preta.relationship_style: You need a lot of reassurance, even if you don't ask for it directly. Your partner may feel like they're constantly being measured. The growth edge is learning that love isn't earned through achievement.
preta.work_style: You're driven and productive, but your motivation comes from comparison rather than purpose. You'll outperform, but you won't enjoy it until you decouple self-worth from external metrics.

pashu.name: Pashu Kaya
pashu.description: Your natural state is rest. Not laziness  - rest. Your body and mind are wired for conservation, for steady rhythms, for doing things at your own pace. The world's urgency often feels manufactured to you, and honestly, you're not wrong.
behavioral_patterns: Your snooze button has seen more action than any other button in your life
You procrastinate not because you're lazy but because urgency doesn't register until the last minute
You can sleep anywhere, anytime  - it's a genuine superpower (and sometimes a genuine problem)
You feel lethargic in the mornings and need a long runway before you're functional
You know you should exercise. You've thought about it. Tomorrow seems like a good day to start
strengths: Doesn't waste energy on unnecessary things
Steady and reliable once momentum builds
Comfortable with slowness in a fast world
Low stress levels compared to peers
shadows: Others mistake your pace for laziness
Drowsiness and lethargy affect daily functioning
pashu.relationship_style: You're easygoing and non-demanding, which many partners appreciate. But your comfort zone can become a prison  - your partner may feel like they're always the one initiating plans, dates, and changes.
pashu.work_style: You do your best work with clear deadlines and external structure. Without it, things drift. You're reliable once you start, but starting is the hard part. Jobs with routine and predictability suit you better than startup chaos.

matsya.name: Matsya Kaya
matsya.description: You move through life cautiously, preferring safety over risk, familiarity over novelty. Fear isn't always loud for you  - sometimes it's just a quiet decision to stay where you are instead of reaching for something new.
behavioral_patterns: You avoid confrontation even when someone has crossed a clear line  - peace at any cost
You defer to others' opinions in group settings, even when you disagree internally
Change  - any change  - triggers anxiety. New job, new city, new routine: all feel threatening
You sometimes feel hopeless about the future, like things won't get better no matter what you do
You'd rather endure a bad situation you know than risk an unknown one that might be worse
shadows: Fear holds you back from growth
matsya.relationship_style: You give a lot in relationships because you fear abandonment. You need a partner who is patient and encouraging  - someone who helps you take small risks without pushing too hard.
matsya.work_style: You do well in supportive, low-conflict environments with clear expectations. You're reliable and thorough, but you won't advocate for promotions or raises. You need a manager who actively supports your growth.

vanaspatya.name: Vanaspatya Kaya
vanaspatya.description: You are deeply rooted, almost immovable. Your mind prefers the familiar, the routine, the unchanging. Mental effort  - learning new things, solving complex problems, engaging in abstract thinking  - feels genuinely exhausting to you.
behavioral_patterns: You forget things that others remember easily  - appointments, conversations, tasks
You avoid social gatherings not because you dislike people, but because the effort of engaging feels enormous
Learning new skills or subjects feels like pushing a boulder uphill
Personal hygiene and self-care slip when you're alone  - you do it for others, not for yourself
You feel indifferent toward goals that excite everyone else  - ambition feels foreign
strengths: Not swayed by trends or social pressure
shadows: Others may underestimate or overlook you
vanaspatya.relationship_style: You need a partner who accepts you without trying to change you, while gently encouraging small steps forward. Your love is quiet and steady, expressed through presence rather than words.
vanaspatya.work_style: Manual, repetitive, or routine work suits you better than creative or intellectual roles. You're reliable for consistent tasks but struggle when the job requires learning, adapting, or socializing.
